text:
Once upon a time in a quaint little village, nestled beneath rolling hills and surrounded by forests filled with dancing lights in the wind, lived a girl named Lena.

Every morning, Lena would awake before dawn, her eyes reflecting the orange glow of the rising sun. She would eat a simple breakfast: maybe a cup of chicory coffee and a slice of honeycake. Then, she'd throw on some comfy shoes and head out into the meadow.

It wasn’t the flowers that made morning special; it was the dew on the roses—a shimmer like stars fallen into a garden bed. Lena would jog around the edges of the meadow, listening to her own cheerful chirping.

One particular morning, she noticed a strange glittering on a stump. Climbing up, she discovered a collection of tiny glass insects swirling in the dew. She caught a beetle, so tiny it could fit in her fist.

Over the weeks, Lena collected these treasures. She named them Glitterbugs. Every evening after
